purpose: Bootstrap module for the core group; creates the base home-directory layout, installs all broad-use apt CLI tools and compilers, creates Debian name-compat symlinks for fd and bat, writes the generated shellrc.sh and wires it into ~/.bashrc, and records all tools in the manifest.
invariants: All apt packages are installed in a single apt_install call — no per-package invocations
core_write_shellrc fully overwrites ~/tools/shellrc.sh on every run; it is always regenerated, never patched
Exactly two PATH additions are written: ~/.local/bin and ~/tools/bin — no per-tool entries
core_link_repo_bins creates symlinks in ~/tools/bin pointing back at the repo's bin/ so git pulls update them in place
fd and bat symlinks are only created if the Debian-named binaries (fdfind, batcat) exist and the target link does not already exist
gotchas: Debian ships fd as fdfind and bat as batcat due to name conflicts — core_binary_aliases creates the conventional-named symlinks; callers relying on fd/bat without running core_binary_aliases will get command-not-found
core_record_manifest only records tools whose binary is present (guarded with has); tools whose binary differs from the apt package name use explicit name:binary pairs in the pairs array
sshpass and expect are recorded separately from the main loop because they have non-standard detect commands
GNU time is recorded as /usr/bin/time (not the shell builtin) — callers must use the full path to distinguish it from the shell built-in time
shellrc.sh uses case/esac PATH guards to avoid duplicate entries on re-source; the guards check for the literal string including colon delimiters

key_functions.name: core_packages
key_functions.summary: Installs all core apt packages in one call: compilers, build tools, shell utilities, and modern CLI replacements

key_functions.name: core_binary_aliases
key_functions.summary: Creates ~/tools/bin/fd -> fdfind and ~/tools/bin/bat -> batcat symlinks to expose conventional names on Debian

key_functions.name: core_write_shellrc
key_functions.summary: Writes ~/tools/shellrc.sh with the two PATH additions and idempotently adds a sourcing line to ~/.bashrc

key_functions.name: core_link_repo_bins
key_functions.summary: Symlinks every file in the repo's bin/ into ~/tools/bin so devtools and smoke-test are on PATH

key_functions.name: core_record_manifest
key_functions.summary: Upserts a manifest entry for each installed core binary, using per-tool detect command overrides where needed
